topStory.title: Show HN: Auto-generated titles and colors for parallel Claude Code sessions

stories.title: Show HN: Auto-generated titles and colors for parallel Claude Code sessions
stories.storyText: I run three to seven Claude Code sessions in parallel terminals, and I kept losing track of which was doing what, especially toward the end of the day when your mind is so tired with the constant context-switching. which-claude-code is a small plugin that adds a title and color to each Claude Code, so you know right away which Claude Code session does what. 1. On every prompt submit, a UserPromptSubmit hook forks a background claude -p --model haiku call that reads the last few transcript turns

topStory.title: Show HN: Trained a 12M transformer on an ML framework we built from scratch

stories.title: Show HN: Trained a 12M transformer on an ML framework we built from scratch
stories.storyText: We’re two second-year CS students, and over the last 4 months we built a ML framework in Rust+CUDA with a TypeScript API on top (and a WebGPU fallback) Our goal is to fully understand the ML stack end-to-end. To force ourselves to use it for something real, we also trained a 12M-parameter transformer with it. We wrote custom CUDA kernels for operations such as flash attention, AdamW, layer norm, and GELU. We also added a WebGPU path so it can still run on machines without NVIDIA GPUs, though tha

topStory.title: Show HN: LangAlpha – what if Claude Code was built for Wall Street?

stories.title: Show HN: LangAlpha – what if Claude Code was built for Wall Street?
stories.storyText: Some technical context on what we ran into building this. MCP tools don't really work for financial data at scale. One tool call for five years of daily prices dumps tens of thousands of tokens into the context window. And data vendors pack dozens of tools into a single MCP server, schemas alone can eat 50k+ tokens before the agent does anything useful. So we auto-generate typed Python modules from the MCP schemas at workspace init and upload them into the sandbox. The agent just imports them li

topStory.title: Show HN: Omi – watches your screen, hears conversations, tells you what to do

stories.title: Show HN: Omi – watches your screen, hears conversations, tells you what to do
stories.storyText: Spent 4 months and built Omi for Desktop, your life architect: It sees your screen, hears your conversations and will advise you on what to do next Basically Cluely + Rewind + Granola + Wisprflow + ChatGPT + Claude in one app I talk to claude/chatgpt 24/7 but I find it frustrating that i have to capture/send screenshots of my screen and that it doesn't help proactively during my work Whenever omi sees something wrong about my workflow, it will send me a proactive notification with advice. topStory.title: Show HN: Bitterbot – A local-first P2P agent mesh with skill trading

stories.title: Show HN: Bitterbot – A local-first P2P agent mesh with skill trading
stories.storyText: We built this because we were tired of centralized agent frameworks that essentially act as expensive API wrappers. The Tech: It’s a local-first mesh using libp2p. Instead of just passing text, agents trade "skills" over a p2p network. This means the mesh is sovereign...it doesn't rely on a central server to function or mediate agent communication. This is where it gets exciting: We have 300+ nodes live and just cleared a 10/10 security audit for the generated code. We’re currently at 460 stars
